name: Chayote Relleno
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Sponge cake or pound cake, crumbled into fine crumbs (plus 2 tablespoons for topping)
1/2 cup Golden sultanas or black raisins
1 cup Softly whipped cream, barely sweetened
recipeInstructions:
1. Steam the chayotes lengthwise for 35 minutes, or until tender. Do not overcook; you don't want the shells to collapse when scooped out.
2. Grind almonds and sugar in a food processor until the almonds are fairly fine.
3. Preheat oven to 375°F.
4. Remove the seeds from the cooked chayotes and scoop out the pulp, leaving a 1/2-inch-thick shell. Set aside.
5. Process the chayote pulp with ground almonds; add eggs and blend to a puree.
6. Add brandy, vanilla, milk or cream, and nutmeg to the mixture. Blend until smooth.
7. Pour the pudding mixture into the chayote shells (about 1 1/2 cups will remain). Place the stuffed shells in a greased baking dish.
8. Sprinkle slivered almonds and reserved cake crumbs over the tops of the stuffed shells.
9. Bake for 30 minutes, then pour any remaining pudding mixture into a greased loaf pan and bake for an additional 25 minutes.
10. Serve warm with whipped cream.
Serves 6 (The pudding loaf serves 4)
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
